notes:
mắc = expensive, costing a lot of money. You use it to describe things like food, clothes, services, prices.

It comes after the noun: N + mắc. In the North, people often say đắt instead; in the South, mắc is very common. Opposite: rẻ (cheap).

Very common extra meanings: mắc lỗi (make a mistake), mắc kẹt (be stuck), mắc nợ (be in debt). Same word, different everyday situations.
exampleSentencesTranslated: This shirt is a bit expensive so I haven't bought it yet.
I think this restaurant is more expensive than other places.
I think those shoes are expensive but very beautiful.

translation.en: The price of this product is very expensive.
The price of this product is very expensive.

notes_md:
**mắc** = expensive, costing a lot of money. You use it to describe things like food, clothes, services, prices.

It comes after the noun: *N + mắc*. In the North, people often say **đắt** instead; in the South, **mắc** is very common. Opposite: **rẻ** (cheap).

Very common extra meanings: **mắc lỗi** (make a mistake), **mắc kẹt** (be stuck), **mắc nợ** (be in debt). Same word, different everyday situations.
exampleSentences: Cái áo này hơi mắc nên em chưa mua được.
Anh thấy nhà hàng này mắc hơn chỗ khác nhiều.
Chị nghĩ đôi giày đó mắc nhưng rất đẹp.

definitions.en: Costing a lot of money; expensive.
definitions.vi: Có giá cao; tốn nhiều tiền.
